summary: Small matching-oriented cleanup in `src/melee/gm/gmadventure.c` for Adventure mode game-mode code. The PR refactored two functions, `gm_801B4860` and `gm_801B4B28`, by splitting portions into `static inline` helpers and improving local variable naming while preserving behavior. One whitespace/indentation-only pragma alignment was also adjusted. Evidence suggests the goal was to make these functions match or remain matched by controlling inlining and local lifetime/order, not to change game logic.
what_changed: `gm_801B4860` was split into two `static inline` helpers, `gm_801B4860_inline0` and `gm_801B4860_inline1`, with the public function now calling those helpers in sequence.
`gm_801B4860_inline0` handles the `MatchExitInfo` result check: if `match_end.result != 1`, it sets `gm_8017E424()->x76 = 1`; otherwise it increments `player_standings[0].stocks`.

`gm_801B4860_inline1` reacquires `MatchExitInfo` and `UnkAdventureData`, calls `setValUnk(scene->idx, exit_info->x8)`, and conditionally calls `gm_8017CBAC` when `gm_8017D7AC(...)` succeeds and `gm_8017E4C4(scene->idx)[1].x0 == 0xFF`.
`gm_801B4B28` was reduced to a call to a new `static inline void inline0(MinorScene* scene)` followed by `gm_801B4064(scene)`.

The extracted `inline0` for `gm_801B4B28` contains the previous Adventure-data setup path guarded by `adv_data->x0.xC.x11 != 1`, including calls to `gm_80169520`, `gm_80169540`, `gm_8017E48C`, `gm_8017E4C4`, `gm_8016A22C`, and setting `adv_data->x0.xC.x11 = 1`.
Temporary-style locals such as `temp_r3`, `temp_r30`, and `temp_r29` were renamed in the refactored sections to more semantic names like `exit_info` and `adv_data`.
A `#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wpointer-to-int-cast"` line near `gm_801B4064` was adjusted for indentation only.
smart_moves: Using `static inline` helper extraction is a practical decomp matching tactic when the compiler's register allocation, variable lifetime boundaries, or scheduling differ for a monolithic function.

The split in `gm_801B4860` deliberately reacquires values in the second inline helper instead of carrying locals across the whole function, which can help reproduce the original codegen around repeated calls to `gm_801A4284(scene)` and `gm_8017E424()`.
Renaming only the locals touched by the refactor to `exit_info` and `adv_data` improved readability without broadening the patch scope.
The public function boundaries remained unchanged for `gm_801B4860` and `gm_801B4B28`, minimizing interface risk while allowing internal codegen manipulation.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn game-mode functions, extracting `static inline` blocks can be used as a non-invasive way to alter generated code while preserving the exported/non-static function surface.
When matching code that originally appears to reacquire global or scene-derived pointers, avoid over-sharing locals across logical blocks; repeated calls and shorter local lifetimes may be necessary.
Semantic local names such as `exit_info` and `adv_data` are preferable once the role of an unknown temporary is clear, especially in functions manipulating `MatchExitInfo` and `UnkAdventureData`.
Keep behavior-preserving matching refactors small and localized. This PR touched only `gmadventure.c` and only the functions needed for the fix.

Inline helper names can be explicitly tied to the parent function, as with `gm_801B4860_inline0` and `gm_801B4860_inline1`, to make matching scaffolding discoverable. The generic `inline0` name used for `gm_801B4B28` is less searchable and may be worth avoiding unless there is a local convention or matching reason.
naming_conventions: Semantic local variable names introduced: `exit_info` for `MatchExitInfo*`, `adv_data` for `UnkAdventureData*`.
Existing address-based function names remain in use for unresolved or not-yet-renamed symbols, such as `gm_801B4860`, `gm_801B4B28`, `gm_8017E424`, and `gm_8017E4C4`.

The diff also introduced a generic `static inline void inline0(MinorScene* scene)` helper inside `gmadventure.c`; evidence does not show whether this was intentional naming convention or a temporary matching artifact.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split a formerly single function body into multiple `static inline` helpers to influence compiler output without changing the external symbol.
Shorten local variable scopes by declaring locals inside extracted inline blocks.
Recompute/reload pointers in separate inline blocks, e.g. calling `gm_801A4284(scene)` and `gm_8017E424()` in `gm_801B4860_inline1` rather than reusing values from the earlier block.
Preserve call order around side-effecting helper calls such as `setValUnk`, `gm_8017D7AC`, `gm_8017CBAC`, and `gm_801B4064`.
Use a local `#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wpointer-to-int-cast"` for a known pointer-to-`u8` cast in `gm_801B4064`; the PR only adjusted indentation there.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to the context counts, but no comment body was included in the excerpt, so no reviewer intent or feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search for other `gmadventure.c` functions that were matched by extracting `static inline` helpers.
Check whether generic helper names like `inline0` are accepted convention in this repository or should be parent-prefixed for searchability.
Inspect nearby Adventure mode functions using `MatchExitInfo` and `UnkAdventureData` for similar repeated-call lifetime patterns.
Verify whether `gm_801B4860` and `gm_801B4B28` are reported as matched after this PR in the build/report artifacts.
